textInfo.title: Elevated GGT
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your Gamma-Glutamyl Transferase (GGT) level is significantly elevated. GGT is an enzyme found in many organs, particularly the liver. High levels can indicate liver or bile duct damage. Your doctor will likely investigate the cause of this elevation.

textInfo.medicalDescription: T2 isointenser Herdläson des Fundus (22 mm, Serie 32, Bild 81 oder Serie 107, Bild 13). Interpretation: Läsion am Uterusfundus, am ehesten subseröses Myom, falls unbekannt weitere Abklärung im Rahmen der regulären gynäkologischen Vorsorge.
textInfo.originalDescription:
T2 isointenser Herdläson des Fundus (22 mm, Serie
32, Bild 81 oder Serie 107, Bild 13
textInfo.title: Probable myom on the uterus
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows a distinct area, or lesion, measuring 22 mm at the top of your uterus, known as the fundus. The radiologist's interpretation suggests this is most likely a subserous myoma, which is a type of benign (non-cancerous) growth also known as a fibroid, located on the outer surface of the uterus.

textInfo.title: Elevated HbA1c (Pre-diabetes range)
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your HbA1c level is 5.8%, which is in the pre-diabetes range (5.7-6.4%). HbA1c reflects your average blood sugar level over the past 2-3 months. This result suggests an increased risk of developing diabetes, and lifestyle changes like diet and exercise are often recommended.

textInfo.title: Elevated Albumin
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your albumin level is slightly elevated. Albumin is a protein made by your liver that helps keep fluid in your bloodstream and carries various substances throughout your body. Mildly elevated levels are often associated with dehydration.

textInfo.title: Elevated ALAT (GPT)
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your Alanine Aminotransferase (ALAT or GPT) level is slightly elevated. ALAT is an enzyme found mostly in the liver. A high level can be a sign of liver inflammation or damage. This is a minor elevation, and your doctor will interpret it in the context of your other results and overall health.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Cortical Gray Matter Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: The normalized cortical gray matter volume is 37.4%, which is above the provided reference range of [31.0% - 36.7%].
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your normalized cortical gray matter volume, which represents the volume of the outer layer of your brain relative to your total intracranial volume, is slightly higher than the typical range for your age and sex. The clinical significance of this finding should be discussed with your doctor.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Right Parietal Lobe Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: Normalized gray matter volume of the right parietal lobe (4.56%) is slightly above the normal range of [3.63 - 4.49]%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of the gray matter in your right parietal lobe, when adjusted for the total size of your brain, is slightly higher than what is typically seen for your age and gender. The measured value is 4.56%, while the normal range is 3.63% to 4.49%.

textInfo.title: Normal brain blood vessels
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI angiography, a special type of scan that visualizes blood vessels, showed that the arteries in your brain appear normal. The radiologist checked for any signs of narrowing (stenosis), ballooning weaknesses in the vessel wall (aneurysm), or blockages. The finding that these vessels are normal ('regelrecht') indicates that no such abnormalities were detected and blood flow appears to be unobstructed.

textInfo.title: Borderline High Visceral Fat Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: Visceral adipose tissue (VAT) volume measured at 4.4 L, which is within the provided normal range of 1.0 - 4.5 L but at the upper limit (89th percentile rank).
textInfo.originalDescription: biomarker_name: VAT volume(L), value: 4.4, unit: L, minNormalValue: 1, maxNormalValue: 4.5, Rank: 89
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your volume of visceral adipose tissue (VAT), the fat surrounding your internal organs, is 4.4 L. This is at the high end of the normal range of 1.0 L to 4.5 L. Elevated visceral fat is associated with increased health risks, and monitoring is advised.

textInfo.title: Increased Fat in Right Psoas Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: Elevated intramuscular fat (IMF) in the right psoas major muscle at 10.4%, which is above the normal reference range of 6.4 - 9.4%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The analysis of your MRI shows an increased amount of fat within your right psoas major muscle, a key muscle in your lower back and hip region. This is known as intramuscular fat (IMF). Your value of 10.4% is slightly above the normal range of 6.4% to 9.4%. Elevated IMF can be a sign of reduced muscle quality and may be associated with back pain or weakness.

textInfo.title: Elevated Left Parietal Lobe Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: Normalized gray matter volume of the left parietal lobe (5.04%) is above the normal range of [3.72 - 4.71]%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of the gray matter in your left parietal lobe, when adjusted for the total size of your brain, is higher than what is typically seen for your age and gender. The measured value is 5.04%, while the normal range is 3.72% to 4.71%.

textInfo.title: Uncomplicated pouches in the colon
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI shows a few small pouches, called diverticula, in the wall of your sigmoid colon, which is part of the large intestine. They are uncomplicated and show no signs of inflammation or infection (a condition known as diverticulitis). This underlying condition, called diverticulosis, is common, particularly with increasing age, and usually does not cause any problems.

textInfo.title: Normal position of the uterus
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows that your uterus is in an anteverted and anteflexed position. This means it is tilted forward towards your bladder and also bent forward on itself. This is the most common position for the uterus and is considered a normal anatomical variation, not a cause for concern.

textInfo.title: Elevated HbA1c (IFCC)
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your HbA1c level, measured by the IFCC standard, is slightly elevated. This test reflects your average blood sugar over the past few months. An elevated result indicates a higher-than-normal average blood sugar, which can be a sign of pre-diabetes.

textInfo.title: Elevated Eosinophils
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your eosinophil count is slightly higher than the normal range. Eosinophils are a type of white blood cell that are often elevated in response to allergies, asthma, or certain infections. This finding is generally minor but may be discussed with your doctor in the context of your overall health.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Right Frontal Lobe Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: Normalized gray matter volume of the right frontal lobe (7.07%) is slightly above the normal range of [5.80 - 7.01]%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of the gray matter in your right frontal lobe, when adjusted for the total size of your brain, is slightly higher than what is typically seen for your age and gender. The measured value is 7.07%, while the normal range is 5.80% to 7.01%.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Pallidum Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: Total pallidum volume, normalized to total intracranial volume, is 0.31%, which is slightly above the normal range of [0.23% - 0.30%].
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of your pallidum, a structure deep within the brain, is 0.31% of your total intracranial volume. This is slightly above the typical range of 0.23% to 0.30% for your age and gender. This is a minor finding and is often of no clinical significance on its own.

textInfo.title: Normal spine findings
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI scan of the spine shows a normal result. The radiologist examined the vertebrae, which are the bones that make up your spinal column, the intervertebral discs that act as cushions between them, and the spinal cord. No abnormalities such as disc herniations, significant wear and tear (degeneration), fractures, or suspicious lesions were found. A normal finding means the structures of your spine appear as expected.

textInfo.title: Elevated Hematocrit
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your hematocrit level is slightly elevated. Hematocrit is the proportion of red blood cells in your blood. An elevated level can be caused by various factors, including dehydration or certain underlying conditions, and may warrant further investigation if persistently high.

textInfo.title: Small simple cysts on both kidneys
textInfo.medicalDescription: Kleine Zysten am linken Nierenunterpol sowie im rechten Nierenunterpol.
textInfo.originalDescription: Kleine Zysten am linken Nierenunterpol sowie im rechten Nierenunterpol.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI scan revealed small, fluid-filled sacs, known as cysts, on the lower parts of both your left and right kidneys. These are described as simple cysts, meaning they have thin walls and contain only clear fluid. Simple kidney cysts are very common, especially as people age, and are almost always benign (non-cancerous). They typically do not cause any symptoms or affect kidney function.

textInfo.title: Elevated Total Cholesterol
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your total cholesterol level is slightly elevated. Total cholesterol is a measure of all the cholesterol in your blood. High cholesterol can increase your risk of cardiovascular disease, and your doctor may discuss lifestyle modifications or other treatments.

textInfo.title: Slightly Elevated Total Abdominal Fat
textInfo.medicalDescription: Total abdominal fat volume is 10.3 L, which is slightly elevated above the normal range of 9.12 - 10.22 L.
textInfo.originalDescription: biomarker_name: Total Abdominal Fat, value: 10.3, unit: L, minNormalValue: 9.12, maxNormalValue: 10.22
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your total abdominal fat volume is 10.3 L, which is slightly above the normal range of 9.12 L to 10.22 L. Elevated abdominal fat can be associated with health risks, so lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
